candidate_llm_application: Brick-and-mortar adapter laminate: alternating hard rotation-locked + soft regularizer + sparse skip-bridge for crack-arrest fine-tune robustness

results.title: Toughening mechanism of nacre (PMC review)
results.snippet: Source-domain: brick-and-mortar + mineral bridges + 3 toughening mechanisms (crack deflection, fiber pull-out, organic matrix bridging).
results.functional_justification: Pure source-domain review of nacre toughening (brick-and-mortar, mineral bridges, crack deflection). No LLM/adapter/PEFT content; cannot be functionally equivalent to the LLM laminate application.

results.title: Origin of flaw-tolerance in nacre (Sci Rep)
results.snippet: Source-domain: flaw tolerance from architecture; alternating-stiffness.
results.functional_justification: Source-domain mechanics paper on nacre flaw tolerance and alternating-stiffness. Materials-science only, no LLM application; not functionally equivalent.

results.title: Mineral bridges of nacre (Acta Mech Sin)
results.functional_justification: Source-domain mechanics of nacre mineral-bridge interface. No LLM/PEFT side; not a functional equivalent of the proposed laminate adapter scheme.

results.title: Colluding LoRA: Composite Attack on LLM Safety (2026/3)
results.snippet: LLM-side: CoLoRA composite attack evading detection.
results.functional_justification: Composite collusion across LoRA modules is an attack-side construction, not a defensive alternating hard/soft laminate with skip-bridges. Some overlap in 'multi-LoRA composition' framing only.

results.title: AdvLoRA: Adversarial Low-Rank Adaptation of VLMs
results.snippet: LLM-side: PEFT for adversarial robustness via LoRA.
results.functional_justification: AdvLoRA targets adversarial robustness via LoRA, sharing the goal of robust PEFT but uses adversarial training rather than alternating-stiffness lamination + sparse skip-bridges; mechanism differs.

results.title: Rethinking LoRA for Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning (2026/2)
results.snippet: LLM-side: alternating-minimization LoRA for federated robustness.
results.functional_justification: Introduces 'alternating' structure but at the optimization (FL aggregation) level, not as a structural hard/soft laminate of adapters. Shares the alternation motif but not the brick-and-mortar topology with skip-bridges.

results.title: Survey on LoRA of LLMs
results.snippet: LLM-side: broad survey covering many LoRA variants.
results.functional_justification: Survey provides coverage of stacked/multi-LoRA designs; may mention compositions or modular schemes but does not specify the alternating hard/soft laminate with nacre-style nanobridges and crack-arrest robustness.

results.title: Activated LoRA: Fine-tuned LLMs for Intrinsics (2025/4)
results.functional_justification: Activation-gated LoRA modulates which adapter fires per input; not a structural hard/soft laminate with sparse skip-bridges for crack-arrest robustness to adversarial fine-tune.

verdict_justification: Zero hits across the 8 results under the frozen OR rule. No source has kw>=2 (all are 0), none reach sem>=0.7, and none reach func>=0.7. The closest LLM-side prior (alternating-minimization LoRA-FL at func=0.60) shares the 'alternation' motif but at the optimization level, not as a structural hard/soft laminate with sparse skip-bridge crack-arrest. The candidate brick-and-mortar adapter laminate is not prefigured by any retrieved work.
